description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline that uses the Docker image $CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE/qemu/$IMAGE:$QEMU_CI_CONTAINER_TAG for both test and build stages. Define a .common_test_job_template in the test stage that runs the script: source scripts/ci/gitlab-ci-section; section_start buildenv "Setting up to run tests"; scripts/git-submodule.sh update roms/SLOF; build/pyvenv/bin/meson subprojects download $(cd build/subprojects && echo *); cd build; find . -type f -exec touch {} +; if [ "x${QEMU_TEST_CACHE_DIR}" != "x" ]; then $MAKE precache-functional ; fi; section_end buildenv; section_start test "Running tests"; $MAKE NINJA=":" $MAKE_CHECK_ARGS; section_end test. Add a .functional_test_job_template that extends the common test job, adds a before_script configuring Avocado (creating ~/.config/avocado/avocado.conf, setting cache_dirs, enabling untrusted code, defining QEMU_TEST_CACHE_DIR), defines a cache with key ${CI_JOB_NAME}-cache and paths avocado‑cache and functional‑cache (policy pull‑push), sets variable QEMU_JOB_AVOCADO=1, runs an after_script that du‑s the cache directories, and publishes artifacts (results.xml, test‑results, functional logs) with a JUnit report and 7‑day expiration. Define a .native_build_job_template in the build stage that uses the same image, runs a before_script to source gitlab-ci-section and compute JOBS=$(expr $(nproc) + 1), caches ccache with key $CI_JOB_NAME, and executes a script that sets CCACHE variables, shows .git size, creates and enters a build directory, zeroes ccache stats, configures QEMU with ../configure --enable-werror --disable-docs --enable-fdt=system ${TARGETS:+--target-list="$TARGETS"} $CONFIGURE_ARGS, optionally runs meson configure for LD_JOBS, builds with $MAKE -j"$JOBS", optionally runs tests with $MAKE $MAKE_CHECK_ARGS, and finally shows ccache stats. Finally, add a .native_test_job_template that extends the common test template and defines test artifacts (testlog.txt and JUnit XML) with a 7‑day expiration. All jobs run on every pipeline unless overridden by external rules.
